title: E-10
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{Droid |type= |image=[[File:E10-DarkTimes5.jpg]] |name=E-10 |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], [[Esseles/Legends|Esseles]][[Dark Times 5|''Dark Times'' 5]] |creator= |manufacturer= |line= |model= |class=[[Protocol droid/Legends|Protocol droid]] |cost= |length= |width= |height= |mass= |gender= |pronouns= |sensor= |plating=[[Color/Legends|Gold]] |armament= |equipment= |affiliation=[[Dezono Qua]] }} {{Quote|A magnificent repast, E-10. You outdid yourself!|Dezono Qua congratulates E-10 on its cooking of Resa Greenbark|}} '''E-10''' was a [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] owned by the wealthy [[Human/Legends|Human]] heir [[Dezono Qua]], with whom the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] lived on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Esseles/Legends|Esseles]]. Under orders from Qua, E-10 prepared and cooked a number of [[Youngling/Legends|young]] [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]] that its master had purchased and wished to eat. After E-10 cooked the [[Nosaurian/Legends|Nosaurian]] child [[Resa Greenbark]], the droid's master congratulated it on the quality of the dish. The meal proved to be the last the droid prepared, however. Shortly after the dish was created, Resa's [[Parent/Legends|father]], [[Bomo Greenbark]], [[Assault on Dezono Qua's villa|arrived]] at Qua's home and destroyed E-10 after it refused to tell him of Resa's fate. ==Biography== {{Quote|Master—you should get to safety—aah!|E-10 warns its master, Dezono Qua, as the Human pushes the droid over|}} By the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], the [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] E-10 belonged to the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Dezono Qua]], the sole heir to the fortune of a wealthy family on his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Esseles/Legends|Esseles]]. E-10 lived on Esseles with its master in his family's large [[Qua family's villa|villa]], a remote fortress-like structure located on top of a [[Mountain/Legends|mountain]] and guarded by a small [[Army/Legends|army]] of [[Droid/Legends|droids]]. Whilst E-10 served Qua, the Human regularly purchased [[Slavery/Legends|enslaved]] [[Youngling/Legends|children]] from a [[Chagrian/Legends|Chagrian]] slaver named [[Orso Meeto]] on the slave world of [[Orvax IV]].[[Dark Times 4|''Dark Times'' 4]] Qua then had these children [[Death/Legends|killed]] and ate them, ordering E-10 to cook them into dishes for him, something at which the droid was fairly proficient. [[File:E10Death-DarkTimes5.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Bomo Greenbark destroyed E-10.]] In 19 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]], about a [[Standard month/Legends|month]] after the [[Declaration of a New Order|inauguration]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], Qua purchased a female [[Nosaurian/Legends|Nosaurian]] child from Meeto named [[Resa Greenbark]]. As per usual, Qua had E-10 create a meal from the child and, after eating her, declared that the meal had been one of E-10's finest. As the droid thanked its master for the compliment and offered him [[Foodstuff/Legends|dessert]], a rumbling distracted the pair. Realizing they were under attack, Qua fled, pushing the droid over in his haste to escape. The villa suffered an [[Assault on Dezono Qua's villa|attack]] from the crew of the [[Maka-Eekai L4000 transport]] ''[[Uhumele]]'', which included the Nosaurian [[Bomo Greenbark]], who had come searching for his daughter, the main ingredient in E-10's latest dish. E-10 remained in the dining room whilst the crew fought their way through the villa's [[Security droid/Legends|security droids]], which they managed to remotely disable after a short period of [[Time/Legends|time]]. With the villa clear, several members of the crew, including Greenbark, reached the dining room, where they found the droid. The Nosaurian questioned E-10 as to his daughter's location, but when the droid claimed Qua would not wish such information to be shared, Greenbark shot E-10 in the head with a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]], destroying the droid. When they discovered the fate of Resa, the crew also killed Qua. ==Characteristics== {{Quote|Where's my daughter? Where's the Nosaurian youngling?''"
"''I'm sure that my master would not wish me to discuss—|Bomo Greenbark question's E-10 before shooting it|}} E-10 was a bipedal protocol droid with [[Color/Legends|golden]]-colored plating across much of its body, although the back of its head and part of its waist had no plating, which revealed the machinery and wires within. The droid's head housed its internal machinery at the back with a transparent covering. E-10 served Dezono Qua loyally, willingly obliging his requests to cook [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] children and, in the case of Resa Greenbark, refusing to give up information regarding the dead child's location when question. The droid was reasonably able in terms of cooking and produced dishes to its master's satisfaction. When the pair's home was attacked, E-10's greatest concern was that its master got to safety, although the droid did not attempt to flee with him. ==Behind the scenes== E-10 first appeared in the [[Dark Times 5|fifth and final issue]] of the [[Star Wars: Dark Times: The Path to Nowhere|''The Path to Nowhere'']] story arc of the ''[[Star Wars: Dark Times]]'' [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series. The issue was illustrated by [[Douglas Wheatley]], written by "Mick Harrison," a pen name of author [[Randy Stradley]],{{InsiderCite|127|Blaster}} and released [[October 10]], [[2007]].{{DarkHorse|url=Comics/13-732/Star-Wars-Dark-Times-5--The-Path-to-Nowhere|text=Star Wars Dark Times 5: The Path to Nowhere}} ==Appearances== *[[Dark Times 5|''Dark Times'' 5]] {{1st}} ==Notes and references== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of E-10}} {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=E-10 |pl=E-10 }} [[Category:Droids with unspecified gender programming]] [[Category:Droids with unspecified pronouns]] [[Category:Protocol droids]]
